extraction.one_line_summary: BOUWMAN survived multiple attacks and 4000m climbing over 200km to win from a seven-rider break that reformed after splits on descents.
tactical_patterns: Trek-Segafredo controlled tempo but refused to chase aggressively, protecting LÓPEZ's 38-second margin without burning matches for complete break suppression.
BOUWMAN dropped with 25km remaining but clawed back on descent, showing descending recovery ability critical for hilly breakaway stages.
DUMOULIN repeatedly distanced then rejoined on descents, suggesting descending strength compensated for climbing weakness in undulating break dynamics.
KÄMNA attempted late split from group behind break, signaling Bora tactical willingness to force GC selection on medium mountain stages.

rider_signals.BOUWMAN Koen: positive: Won despite being dropped 25km out, revealing exceptional descending recovery and final kick on 13% gradient finish
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: neutral: Dropped multiple times but rejoined via descents, fourth place suggests selective climbing deterioration but intact finishing speed
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: Trek protected 38-second lead through 4000m climbing without aggressive chase, conserving team resources early race
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: neutral: Attempted to split GC group behind break, indicating Bora willing to test GC riders on medium mountain stages
rider_signals.VILLELLA Davide: negative: Dropped from break before final climbs despite Cofidis support, lacking horsepower for sustained breakaway racing
